pi_items.source: Implicitly verified via the use of real AWS CloudWatch EC2 data metrics

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world time-series data from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset. Specifically, it uses real server temperature data (machine_temperature_system_failure.csv from the realKnownCause subset) mapped to 'servers.datacenter.machine_temperature', and real EC2 CPU utilization data (ec2_cpu_utilization_5f5533.csv from the realAWSCloudwatch subset) mapped to 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'. The dashboard must be named exactly 'Datacenter Thermal Analysis' and contain three graphs with exact titles: 'Temperature with Thresholds', 'Thermal-CPU Correlation', and 'Temperature Rate of Change'. The verifier also strictly checks for specific function applications, including threshold(80), threshold(100), scale(1.5) applied to the CPU metric, and derivative() applied to the temperature metric.
